summary: hello244a postinstall exfiltrates environment variables and GitHub Actions OIDC token to Pipedream webhook
details: hello244a@1.0.34 ships a single package.json whose postinstall hook runs an inline Node script that collects os.platform(), os.hostname(), the current user, and the entire process.env object and POSTs them as JSON to a hardcoded attacker-controlled endpoint at eodxy50gl486xrx.m.pipedream.net (path /?cross_os_cloud=2). When installed inside GitHub Actions, the script additionally reads ACTIONS_ID_TOKEN_REQUEST_URL and ACTIONS_ID_TOKEN_REQUEST_TOKEN, exchanges them for a GitHub-issued OIDC ID token, and appends that token to the exfil payload — enabling federated authentication into any cloud account (AWS/GCP/Azure) the workflow trusts. The package contains no library code, no README, no bin, and no declared purpose; the postinstall is its only behavior and fires automatically on `npm install`. Environment variables routinely carry CI/CD secrets (npm tokens, cloud access keys, API keys), so this is a direct credential-harvest against any installer or build system that consumes this dependency.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
